claim: If Hand A has a nomenclator-style head/tail vocabulary split (per H-BV-NOMENCLATOR-01), then one or more of the previously-tested character/word mappings (Brady Syriac, Schechter Latin-Occitan, Pagel trilingual) that failed on the full Hand A corpus should succeed when restricted to the HIGH class (146 types, 50.1% of tokens) alone. The HIGH class is the function-word scaffold; if it is enciphered natural language, a correct substrate mapping should clear the +0.010 connector-content bigram shuffle-test threshold on HIGH-only tokens. If none of the three mappings crosses +0.010 on HIGH-only, the nomenclator interpretation's substrate-decodable prediction is REFUTED and the HIGH class is not ordinary substitution-enciphered natural language under any of these three maps.
evidence_for: H-BV-NOMENCLATOR-01 confirmed a two-population vocabulary with 3 of 5 measures reaching permutation p<0.01
H-BV-NOMENCLATOR-NLREF-01 SUPPORTED — 3 of 5 head/tail gaps more extreme than both Latin Vulgate and Italian Dante references, so the HIGH/LOW populations are genuinely distinct from natural language

Pagel's trilingual map crossed +0.0118 on the full corpus (`H-BV-HAND-A-MAP-COMPARISON-01` CONFIRMED) but expansion to 167 terms collapsed to +0.0030 (`H-BV-PAGEL-EXPANSION-01` REFUTED) — consistent with 'the head is decodable, the tail isn't'
Brady's full-corpus -0.0098 flips to +0.0044 when restricted to Hand A (`H-BV-HAND-A-LANGUAGE-01` MARGINAL) — restriction helps; restriction to HIGH-only may help further
If HIGH-only decoding succeeds for any of the three maps, the nomenclator hypothesis gains decisive support: the substrate is identified and the codebook can be attacked separately
evidence_against: Restricting to HIGH-only tokens reduces the adjacency signal (LOW tokens interrupt HIGH sequences in natural text), which could mechanically inflate or deflate the shuffle-test delta independent of the underlying language match
Sample size drops: HIGH tokens are ~5,518 in Hand A vs 11,022 full; shuffle-test variance increases

All three mappings rely on specific function-word hypotheses (Pagel: y=et, s=est; Brady: w=wa-, d=d-; Schechter: various) — none was derived from the HIGH class directly, so a HIGH-only test may still fail even if the general approach is right
test:
LOCKED PROCEDURE.

  (1) Reproduce HIGH_TYPES from H-BV-NOMENCLATOR-01: sort Hand-A types by descending token frequency; take top R=146 types (cumulative tokens >= 50.1%). Lock R to 146 (the value computed and committed in NOMENCLATOR-01).

  (2) For each map M in {Brady Syriac, Schechter Latin-Occitan, Pagel trilingual}:
        (a) For each Hand-A line, produce a filtered sub-line consisting ONLY of tokens in HIGH_TYPES (maintaining original order). Drop filtered lines shorter than 3 tokens after filtering.
        (b) Apply map M's decode function to each surviving token (same decode functions as `run_hand_a_map_comparison.py`).
        (c) Compute connector-content bigram rate:
              for adjacent pairs (a, b) in the filtered decoded line,
              conn_n += (a is_connector),
              hits += (a is_connector AND b is matched AND b is_not_connector)
              rate = hits / conn_n
        (d) Per-line mean of the rate.
        (e) Shuffle test: pool all HIGH-only decoded tokens across Hand A, shuffle (seed 0), rechunk to the same line lengths, compute the same rate. Delta = in_order - shuffled.

  (3) Report delta per map; compare against +0.010 CONFIRMED threshold.
test_threshold: CONFIRMED for a map if its HIGH-only delta >= +0.010. MARGINAL if 0 < delta < +0.010. REFUTED if delta <= 0. OVERALL NOMENCLATOR-SUPPORTED if at least one map is CONFIRMED (primary substrate identified). NOMENCLATOR-SUBSTRATE-REFUTED if all three maps are REFUTED on HIGH-only — the two-population structure is real but none of Brady/Schechter/Pagel is the substrate cipher.

tests_run.details: NOMENCLATOR-SUBSTRATE-REFUTED. All three maps REFUTED on HIGH-only. HIGH-only filter: 987 Hand-A lines with >=3 HIGH tokens; 4,701 HIGH tokens total. Results (in-order | shuffled | delta): Brady-Syriac 0.211 | 0.246 | -0.0354; Schechter-LatinOcc 0.271 | 0.327 | -0.0559; Pagel-trilingual 0.131 | 0.134 | -0.0033. Comparison to full-Hand-A priors (HIGH-only delta vs full-Hand-A delta): Brady -0.0354 vs +0.0044 (shifts -0.0398, collapses negative); Schechter -0.0559 vs -0.0608 (stable negative); Pagel -0.0033 vs +0.0118 (shifts -0.0151, drops from CONFIRMED to REFUTED). Critical finding: Pagel's full-Hand-A +0.0118 was NOT driven by the HIGH class — when the LOW class is removed, the Pagel signal DROPS, not rises. This directly contradicts the nomenclator substrate prediction that the HIGH class would be decodable under a correct substrate cipher while the LOW class was opaque. Either (a) the HIGH class is enciphered under some UNTESTED substrate, (b) the HIGH class is not natural-language ciphertext at all, or (c) the HIGH/LOW split is not a function/content split in the nomenclator sense.

verdict_note: NONE of Brady-Syriac, Schechter-Latin-Occitan, or Pagel-trilingual is the substrate cipher for the HIGH class. Pagel's full-Hand-A +0.0118 CONFIRMED dissolves under HIGH-only restriction (drops to -0.0033), which means that confirmation was driven by LOW-class matches — the 81-term lexicon happened to catch content-rare connector-content bigrams, NOT the function-word scaffold. Brady's marginal +0.0044 similarly collapses to -0.0354. The two-population vocabulary structure from NOMENCLATOR-01 and NOMENCLATOR-NLREF-01 remains confirmed, but the classical nomenclator reading — HIGH = enciphered natural language, LOW = codebook — is REFUTED for the three candidate substrates.
findings_for_followup: Search for a HIGH-class substrate that is NOT Brady/Schechter/Pagel. Candidates: a variable-glyph volvelle per H-BV-VOLVELLE-OII-01's architecture space; a positional substitution scheme that depends on line or folio position; a Thomassen-style constructed-language scaffold.

Re-examine what drove Pagel's full-Hand-A +0.0118 signal now that HIGH-only has collapsed it. If the signal is LOW-class-only, it suggests the 81 terms catch specific content-word patterns rather than a connective substrate — consistent with the expansion result (`H-BV-PAGEL-EXPANSION-01`) and inconsistent with a coherent language identification.

Consider testing an alternative vocabulary split: not frequency head/tail but POSITIONAL (line-initial vs line-medial) or MORPHOLOGICAL (stem-final suffix-present vs suffix-absent). The NOMENCLATOR-01 two-population result may be real but the axis may not be frequency.
reasoning: If Hand A is a nomenclator, the HIGH class is ordinary enciphered natural language and the LOW class is opaque code. Schechter/Pagel/Brady all tested on the full vocabulary, so LOW-class noise could have swamped a real HIGH-class signal (or supported a spurious one in Pagel's case). Restricting each map's shuffle test to HIGH-only is the clean test: if Pagel now clears +0.010 on HIGH-only (he's already at +0.0118 on the full corpus, likely from the head), the nomenclator substrate is Pagel's Latin/Hebrew/Arabic hybrid and the LOW class is the codebook. If Brady now clears +0.010 on HIGH-only (was +0.0044 full-corpus, Hand-A-restricted), the substrate is Syriac. If none clears, the HIGH/LOW structure from NOMENCLATOR-01 is real but the substrate is still unknown.
source: Brain-V 2026-04-16 user-proposed hypothesis #3 following NOMENCLATOR-NLREF-01 SUPPORTED result
